markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Add breadcrumbs across project, folder, doc, block, symbol, WP, MT, and source spans. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: NavigationBreadcrumbs leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: NavigationBreadcrumbs is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Backend breadcrumb API across the LoomBlock entity spine (workspace -> project -> folder ancestry -> block -> ProjectKnowledgeIndex entity). Reuses MT-181 folders + MT-177 bridge. Read projection. The remaining spine kinds (doc/symbol/WP/MT/source-span) are surfaced by the frontend over the existing knowledge entity/edge graph; the block/folder/project/entity backbone is delivered here.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - impl: workspace crumb -> project crumb (root-most ancestry folder project_ref) -> folder ancestry (cycle-guarded recursive CTE, root-first) -> block crumb -> entity crumb (when bridged); deterministic single folder branch

tests: breadcrumbs_span_workspace_folder_ancestry_block_and_entity - workspace first, project crumb from Work.project_ref, folder ancestry root-first (Work, Projects), block crumb with title, entity crumb last
breadcrumbs_for_unfiled_unbridged_block_are_minimal - [workspace, block] only when no folder + not bridged

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): 1a5f3abb.

findings: PROJECT-REF FROM ANCESTRY (fixed during impl): the project crumb originally read project_ref from the leaf folder only; a block in a child folder whose PARENT carries the project_ref got no project crumb. Fixed by scanning the whole ancestry (root-most project_ref wins). Proven by the nested-folder test.
FOLDER CYCLE (handled): the ancestry recursive CTE carries a path array + excludes visited folders, so a malformed cyclic tree cannot loop.
MULTI-FOLDER MEMBERSHIP (documented+handled): a block can be in several folders; one deterministic branch (first by created_at, folder_id) is chosen for the trail.
NO ABSOLUTE PATHS (clear): crumbs carry stable ids/labels (workspace id/name, folder id/name, block id/title, entity id), never filesystem paths (MT-152 discipline).
FAIL-CLOSED (tested): a missing block errors; the entity crumb only appears when the block is bridged.
fixes: Pull the project crumb from the root-most ancestry folder carrying a project_ref instead of the leaf folder only.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
